'''Mantoid''' was a combat [[Droid/Legends|droid]] gladiator in [[the Cauldron]] on [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]]. During the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], Mantoid was destroyed by gladiator [[Grunda Dolma]] in close combat.

==Biography==
[[File:DolmaDestroyingMantoid.jpg|280px|left|thumb|Grunda Dolma destroys Mantoid in The Cauldron arena]]
During the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], a combat [[Droid/Legends|droid]],<ref name="CE" /> Mantoid,<ref name="Sticker">''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''</ref> made the rounds in the [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] arenas of the planet [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]].<ref name="CE" /> Eventually, shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> this droid ended up in [[the Cauldron]].<ref name="CW" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on Rattatak, filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants to win their wars.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, like many other gladiators, Mantoid entered<ref name="CW" /> a deadly free-for-all battle,<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> where only a rare combatant survived multiple fights.<ref name="DBR" /> During one battle, Mantoid encountered the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Grunda Dolma]], who wielded a double-headed [[Staff/Legends|staff]]. Despite its numerous weapons, the droid was slower than Dolma, which allowed the Aqualish to deliver a fatal blow.<ref name="CW" />

==Characteristics==
An unusual combat [[Droid/Legends|droid]]<ref name="CE" /> composed of cylindrical black body with an oval head. The skinny, multiarmed Mantoid was armed with a variety of bladed and other weapons.<ref name="CW" /><ref name="CE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mantoid<ref name="Sticker" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The droid was later given a name in ''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', a sticker book written by [[Simon Beecroft]] and released in [[2004]].<ref name="DK">{{DK|url=us/9780756607593-ultimate-sticker-book-star-wars-clone-wars|text=Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars|nolive=1}}</ref>
